entities.name: COTTON
entities.description: The primary crop for which insecticide resistance management practices were developed in India

entities.name: COTTON BOLLWORM
entities.description: Helicoverpa armigera, a major pest of cotton monitored for insecticide resistance

entities.name: PINK BOLLWORM
entities.description: A pest of cotton present in the late season

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the cotton ICM programmes and resistance monitoring were implemented

entities.name: INSECTICIDE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pests like H. armigera to survive exposure to insecticides, acting as a constraint to cotton production

entities.name: DISCRIMINATING DOSE ASSAYS
entities.description: The method used to routinely monitor resistance levels of cotton bollworm to various insecticides

entities.name: INTEGRATED CROP MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A strategy designed to maximize profit while minimizing insecticide use and the impact of resistance

entities.name: SEED TREATMENT
entities.description: An agronomic practice used to delay the first foliar insecticide application

entities.name: PYRETHROID
entities.description: A class of insecticides to which cotton bollworm resistance was monitored

entities.name: ORGANOPHOSPHATE
entities.description: A class of insecticides to which cotton bollworm resistance was monitored

entities.name: CARBAMATE
entities.description: A class of insecticides to which cotton bollworm resistance was monitored

entities.name: CYCLODIENE
entities.description: A class of insecticides to which cotton bollworm resistance was monitored

entities.name: ENDOSULFAN
entities.description: A specific insecticide used as the first rotation in the ICM strategy

entities.name: VILLAGE PARTICIPATORY TRIALS
entities.description: The method used to demonstrate the ICM system across 24 villages in India
